steps.title: Find the input value
steps.content:
Use the linear equation to find the shared $x$-coordinate.

$$x+6=16$$

$$x=10$$

steps.title: Evaluate the output
steps.content: Substitute $x=10$ into the quadratic equation to find the matching $y$-coordinate. $$y=3(10)^2+4$$ $$y=3(100)+4$$ $$y=304$$ The intersection point is $(10{,}304)$. The answer is C.
